channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Flood sirens blare in South Central Texas as rivers reach perilous heights
item.description: Flood sirens blared early Thursday in Comfort, Texas, as the Guadalupe River rose more than 30 feet in three hours, the U.S. Geological Survey said. The storm could bring even more rainfall than last year's deadly Fourth of July floods.
item.content:encoded:
Flood sirens blared early Thursday in Comfort, Texas, as the Guadalupe River rose more than 30 feet in three hours, the U.S. Geological Survey said. The storm could bring even more rainfall than last year's deadly Fourth of July floods.

(Image credit: Brandon Bell)
item.dc:creator: Bill Chappell

item.title: Officials probe whether White House teleprompter operator profited off Trump's words
item.description: It's the first known instance of officials investigating suspected insider trading on a prediction market from inside the White House.
item.content:encoded:
It's the first known instance of officials investigating suspected insider trading on a prediction market from inside the White House.

(Image credit: Justin Sullivan)
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn

item.title: Trump wants a permanent fence around the D.C. park known for White House protests
item.description: The Trump administration wants to install permanent fencing around Lafayette Park, directly outside the White House. It's long been a popular spot for protesters, who worry barriers will change that.
item.content:encoded:
The Trump administration wants to install permanent fencing around Lafayette Park, directly outside the White House. It's long been a popular spot for protesters, who worry barriers will change that.

(Image credit: U.S. Commission of Fine Arts)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: Hegseth announces plan to screen military members' testosterone levels
item.description: Secretary Pete Hegseth says members of the military over 30 years old should be screened for low testosterone, but what is the evidence for doing this?
item.content:encoded: Secretary Pete Hegseth says members of the military over 30 years old should be screened for low testosterone, but what is the evidence for doing this?
item.dc:creator: Will Stone

item.title: Oil companies are making billions. In the U.S., calls to tax their windfall are growing
item.description: Higher oil prices since the Iran war began mean many oil companies have brought in excess profits. Some U.S. lawmakers want to tax those windfall profits and give the money to lower-income Americans.
item.content:encoded:
Higher oil prices since the Iran war began mean many oil companies have brought in excess profits. Some U.S. lawmakers want to tax those windfall profits and give the money to lower-income Americans.

(Image credit: Brandon Bell)
item.dc:creator: Julia Simon

item.title: The use of force has become a 'default tool' for ICE officers, a new report finds
item.description: An ACLU report looks at widespread use of force in immigration enforcement in the first year of President Trump's return to office. Recent fatal shootings in Texas and Maine have renewed scrutiny on the agency's tactics.
item.content:encoded:
An ACLU report looks at widespread use of force in immigration enforcement in the first year of President Trump's return to office. Recent fatal shootings in Texas and Maine have renewed scrutiny on the agency's tactics.

(Image credit: Scott Olson)
item.dc:creator: Meg Anderson

item.title: Bike lanes and speed cameras disappear from the DOT's list of proven safety measures
item.description: The Federal Highway Administration quietly stripped bike lanes and other safety recommendations from a key list of best practices. Critics say those measures are proven to cut crashes and save lives.
item.content:encoded:
The Federal Highway Administration quietly stripped bike lanes and other safety recommendations from a key list of best practices. Critics say those measures are proven to cut crashes and save lives.

(Image credit: Heather Diehl)
item.dc:creator: Joel Rose

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: Attorney general nominee faces tough questions during confirmation hearing, Trump's notice to resume Iran war comes at tense political moment, director of national security nominee faces lawmakers.
item.content:encoded: Attorney general nominee faces tough questions during confirmation hearing, Trump's notice to resume Iran war comes at tense political moment, director of national security nominee faces lawmakers.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: Treasury unveils commemorative coin featuring Trump's face
item.description: The Treasury has unveiled collectible $1 coins in honor of America's 250th birthday. One side is engraved with President Trump's face, despite laws prohibiting a living person's likeness on currency.
item.content:encoded: The Treasury has unveiled collectible $1 coins in honor of America's 250th birthday. One side is engraved with President Trump's face, despite laws prohibiting a living person's likeness on currency.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: How to dress for the office in sweltering temperatures
item.description: NPR's Michel Martin speaks with Johnny C. Taylor, the CEO of the Society for Human Resource Management, about what to wear to the office in a heatwave.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Michel Martin speaks with Johnny C. Taylor, the CEO of the Society for Human Resource Management, about what to wear to the office in a heatwave.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin
